revision.text: 兜塚古墳 (横浜市)
[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]]

[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] {{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}
'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, there is a legend associated with the {{ill|15th-century|ja|15世紀}} military commander {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}}.
== Location and Overview ==
In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the {{ill|Tsurumi River|ja|鶴見川}}, multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群}} were once distributed.
The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Yokoana Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群}}.
Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''.
== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==
In the {{ill|15th century|ja|15世紀}}, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}} was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for {{ill|Edo Castle|ja|江戸城}}. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台}} (Kaseyama) in present-day {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|ja|川崎市}} and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a {{ill|white heron|ja|白鷺}} flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.
Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).
== Current Status ==
Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}, cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group=note}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Yokohama City History Museum|title=The Kofun and Burial Goods of Yokohama|chapter=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun and Others|publisher=Yokohama Furusato History Foundation|year=2001|page=40}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Yokohama City Board of Education|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|chapter=Tsurumi Ward|page=7|year=2004}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|last=Matsuzaki|first=Motoki|chapter=Characteristics and Regional Structure of Late and Terminal Kofun Groups in the Tama River Basin|title=Late Kofun Groups in the Kanto Region|publisher=Rokuichi Shobo|year=2007|pages=103–122}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Property Center|title=The Kofun Groups Around Komaoka|journal=Maibun Yokohama|issue=29|year=2014|pages=1–4}}
== External Links == * [http://wwwm.city.yokohama.lg.jp/index.asp?dtp=9 Yokohama City Cultural Property Information System "Bunkazai HamaSite"]

[[Category:History of Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Kofun in Yokohama]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Ōta Dōkan]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Yokohama)]][[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Yokohama)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Yokohama)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Yokohama)]][[Category:円墳]]

[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] {{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}
'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, there is a legend associated with the {{ill|15th-century|ja|15世紀}} military commander {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}}.
== Location and Overview ==
In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the {{ill|Tsurumi River|ja|鶴見川}}, multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群}} were once distributed.
The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Yokoana Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群}}.
Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''.
== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==
In the {{ill|15th century|ja|15世紀}}, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}} was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for {{ill|Edo Castle|ja|江戸城}}. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台}} (Kaseyama) in present-day {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|ja|川崎市}} and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a {{ill|white heron|ja|白鷺}} flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.
Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).
== Current Status ==
Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}, cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group=note}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Yokohama City History Museum|title=The Kofun and Burial Goods of Yokohama|chapter=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun and Others|publisher=Yokohama Furusato History Foundation|year=2001|page=40}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Yokohama City Board of Education|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|chapter=Tsurumi Ward|page=7|year=2004}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|last=Matsuzaki|first=Motoki|chapter=Characteristics and Regional Structure of Late and Terminal Kofun Groups in the Tama River Basin|title=Late Kofun Groups in the Kanto Region|publisher=Rokuichi Shobo|year=2007|pages=103–122}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Property Center|title=The Kofun Groups Around Komaoka|journal=Maibun Yokohama|issue=29|year=2014|pages=1–4}}
== External Links == * [http://wwwm.city.yokohama.lg.jp/index.asp?dtp=9 Yokohama City Cultural Property Information System "Bunkazai HamaSite"]

In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the {{ill|Tsurumi River|ja|鶴見川}}, multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} were once distributed.
The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群}}.
Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''.
== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==
In the {{ill|15th century|ja|15世紀}}, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}} was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for {{ill|Edo Castle|ja|江戸城}}. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台}} (Kaseyama) in present-day {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|ja|川崎市}} and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a {{ill|white heron|ja|白鷺}} flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.
Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).
== Current Status ==
Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}, cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

revision.text:
[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] {{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}
'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, there is a legend associated with the {{ill|15th-century|ja|15世紀}} military commander {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}}.
== Location and Overview ==
In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the {{ill|Tsurumi River|ja|鶴見川}}, multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} were once distributed.
The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群}}.
Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''.
== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==
In the {{ill|15th century|ja|15世紀}}, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}} was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for {{ill|Edo Castle|ja|江戸城}}. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台}} (Kaseyama) in present-day {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|ja|川崎市}} and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a {{ill|white heron|ja|白鷺}} flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.
Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).
== Current Status ==
Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}, cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group=note}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Yokohama City History Museum|title=The Kofun and Burial Goods of Yokohama|chapter=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun and Others|publisher=Yokohama Furusato History Foundation|year=2001|page=40}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Yokohama City Board of Education|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|chapter=Tsurumi Ward|page=7|year=2004}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|last=Matsuzaki|first=Motoki|chapter=Characteristics and Regional Structure of Late and Terminal Kofun Groups in the Tama River Basin|title=Late Kofun Groups in the Kanto Region|publisher=Rokuichi Shobo|year=2007|pages=103–122}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Property Center|title=The Kofun Groups Around Komaoka|journal=Maibun Yokohama|issue=29|year=2014|pages=1–4}}
== External Links == * [http://wwwm.city.yokohama.lg.jp/index.asp?dtp=9 Yokohama City Cultural Property Information System "Bunkazai HamaSite"]

[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] {{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}
'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, there is a legend associated with the {{ill|15th-century|ja|15世紀}} military commander {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}}.
== Location and Overview ==
In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the {{ill|Tsurumi River|ja|鶴見川}}, multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} were once distributed.
The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群}}.
Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''.
== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==
In the {{ill|15th century|ja|15世紀}}, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|ja|太田道灌}} was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for {{ill|Edo Castle|ja|江戸城}}. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台}} (Kaseyama) in present-day {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|ja|川崎市}} and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a {{ill|white heron|ja|白鷺}} flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.
Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).
== Current Status ==
Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as {{ill|magatama|ja|勾玉}}, cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group=note}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Yokohama City History Museum|title=The Kofun and Burial Goods of Yokohama|chapter=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun and Others|publisher=Yokohama Furusato History Foundation|year=2001|page=40}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Yokohama City Board of Education|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|chapter=Tsurumi Ward|page=7|year=2004}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|last=Matsuzaki|first=Motoki|chapter=Characteristics and Regional Structure of Late and Terminal Kofun Groups in the Tama River Basin|title=Late Kofun Groups in the Kanto Region|publisher=Rokuichi Shobo|year=2007|pages=103–122}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Property Center|title=The Kofun Groups Around Komaoka|journal=Maibun Yokohama|issue=29|year=2014|pages=1–4}}
== External Links == * [http://wwwm.city.yokohama.lg.jp/index.asp?dtp=9 Yokohama City Cultural Property Information System "Bunkazai HamaSite"]

{{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}

'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|qq=|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the [[:en:6th century|6th century]], there is a legend associated with the [[:en:15th century|15th-century]] military commander [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]].

== Location and Overview ==

In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|qq=|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]], multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.

The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|qq=|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|qq=|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|qq=|1=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|qq=|1=Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}. 

Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. 

== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==

In the [[:en:15th century|15th century]], [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]] was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki, Kanagawa]] and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a [[:en:Egret|white heron]] flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.

Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).

== Current Status ==

Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|qq=|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

{{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}

'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|qq=|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the [[:en:6th century|6th century]], there is a legend associated with the [[:en:15th century|15th-century]] military commander [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]].

== Location and Overview ==

In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|qq=|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]], multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.

The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|qq=|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|qq=|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|qq=|1=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|qq=|1=Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}. 

Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. 

== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==

In the [[:en:15th century|15th century]], [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]] was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki, Kanagawa]] and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a [[:en:Egret|white heron]] flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.

Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).

== Current Status ==

Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|qq=|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.

Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).

== Current Status ==

Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

{{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}

'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the [[:en:6th century|6th century]], there is a legend associated with the [[:en:15th century|15th-century]] military commander [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]].

== Location and Overview ==

In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]], multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.

The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|1=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|1=Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}. 

Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. 

== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==

In the [[:en:15th century|15th century]], [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]] was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki, Kanagawa]] and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a [[:en:Egret|white heron]] flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.

Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).

== Current Status ==

Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).

== Current Status ==

Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tsurumi-ku|WD=Q1202820}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.53438888888889|N|139.6618611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q119218468]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}

'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the [[:en:6th century|6th century]], there is a legend associated with the [[:en:15th century|15th-century]] military commander [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]].

== Location and Overview ==

In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]], multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.

The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|1=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|1=Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}. 

Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. 

== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==

In the [[:en:15th century|15th century]], [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]] was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki, Kanagawa]] and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a [[:en:Egret|white heron]] flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.

Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).

== Current Status ==

Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tsurumi-ku|WD=Q1202820}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.53438888888889|N|139.6618611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q119218468]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}

'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the [[:en:6th century|6th century]], there is a legend associated with the [[:en:15th century|15th-century]] military commander [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]].

== Location and Overview ==

In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]], multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.

The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|1=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|1=Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}. 

Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. 

== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==

In the [[:en:15th century|15th century]], [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]] was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki, Kanagawa]] and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a [[:en:Egret|white heron]] flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.

Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).

== Current Status ==

Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

{{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}

'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the [[:en:6th century|6th century]], there is a legend associated with the [[:en:15th century|15th-century]] military commander [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]].

== Location and Overview ==

In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]], multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.

The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|1=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|1=Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}. 

Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. 

== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==

In the [[:en:15th century|15th century]], [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]] was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki, Kanagawa]] and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a [[:en:Egret|white heron]] flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.

Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).

== Current Status ==

Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

{{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}

'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the [[:en:6th century|6th century]], there is a legend associated with the [[:en:15th century|15th-century]] military commander [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]].

== Location and Overview ==

In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]], multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.

The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|1=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|1=Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}. 

Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. 

== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==

In the [[:en:15th century|15th century]], [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]] was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki, Kanagawa]] and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a [[:en:Egret|white heron]] flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.

Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).

== Current Status ==

Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tsurumi-ku|WD=Q1202820}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.53438888888889|N|139.6618611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q119218468]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q119218468]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}
'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the [[:en:6th century|6th century]], there is a legend associated with the [[:en:15th century|15th-century]] military commander [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]].
== Location and Overview ==
In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the [[:en:Tsurumi River|Tsurumi River]], multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.
The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|1=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|1=Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}.
Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''.
== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==
In the [[:en:15th century|15th century]], [[:en:Ōta Dōkan|Ōta Dōkan]] was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for [[:en:Edo Castle|Edo Castle]]. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki, Kanagawa]] and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a [[:en:Egret|white heron]] flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.
Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).
== Current Status ==
Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as [[:en:Magatama|magatama]], cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

During his sleep, he dreamt of a {{ill|Egret|en|Egret|WD=Q3060012|lt=white heron}} flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.
Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).
== Current Status ==
Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}}, cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tsurumi-ku|WD=Q1202820}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.53438888888889|N|139.6618611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}}}
{{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}
'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, there is a legend associated with the {{ill|15th century|en|15th century|WD=Q7018|lt=15th-century}} military commander {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan}}.
== Location and Overview ==
In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the {{ill|Tsurumi River|en|Tsurumi River|WD=Q7850292|lt=Tsurumi River}}, multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.
The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|1=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|1=Iwaseyama Cave Tomb Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}.
Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''.
== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==
In the {{ill|15th century|en|15th century|WD=Q7018|lt=15th century}}, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan}} was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle}}. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|en|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|WD=Q164234|lt=Kawasaki, Kanagawa}} and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a {{ill|Egret|en|Egret|WD=Q3060012|lt=white heron}} flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.
Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).
== Current Status ==
Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}}, cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

In the {{ill|15th century|en|15th century|WD=Q7018|lt=15th century}}, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan}} was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle}}. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|en|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|WD=Q164234|lt=Kawasaki, Kanagawa}} and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a {{ill|Egret|en|Egret|WD=Q3060012|lt=white heron}} flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.
Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).
== Current Status ==
Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}}, cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group=note}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Yokohama City History Museum|title=The Kofun and Burial Goods of Yokohama|chapter=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun and Others|publisher=Yokohama Furusato History Foundation|year=2001|page=40}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|author=Yokohama City Board of Education|title=Yokohama City Cultural Property Map|chapter=Tsurumi Ward|page=7|year=2004}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|last=Matsuzaki|first=Motoki|chapter=Characteristics and Regional Structure of Late and Terminal Kofun Groups in the Tama River Basin|title=Late Kofun Groups in the Kanto Region|publisher=Rokuichi Shobo|year=2007|pages=103–122}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=Yokohama City Buried Cultural Property Center|title=The Kofun Groups Around Komaoka|journal=Maibun Yokohama|issue=29|year=2014|pages=1–4}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:兜塚古墳 (横浜市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tsurumi-ku|WD=Q1202820}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.53438888888889|N|139.6618611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}}}
{{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}
'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, there is a legend associated with the {{ill|15th century|en|15th century|WD=Q7018|lt=15th-century}} military commander {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan}}.
== Location and Overview ==
In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the {{ill|Tsurumi River|en|Tsurumi River|WD=Q7850292|lt=Tsurumi River}}, multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabos|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.
The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|1=Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Yokoanabo Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Yokoanabo Group|1=Iwaseyama Yokoanabo Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}.
Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''.
== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==
In the {{ill|15th century|en|15th century|WD=Q7018|lt=15th century}}, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan}} was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle}}. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|en|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|WD=Q164234|lt=Kawasaki, Kanagawa}} and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a {{ill|Egret|en|Egret|WD=Q3060012|lt=white heron}} flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.
Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).
== Current Status ==
Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}}, cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.
