== Location and Overview ==
In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the {{ill|Tsurumi River|en|Tsurumi River|WD=Q7850292|lt=Tsurumi River|1=Tsurumi River}}, multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabos|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.
The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|1=Q119218105|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Yokoanabo Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Yokoanabo Group|1=Q119218019|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}.
Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''.
== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==
In the {{ill|15th century|en|15th century|WD=Q7018|lt=15th century|1=15th century}}, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan|1=Ōta Dōkan}} was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle|1=Edo Castle}}. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|en|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|WD=Q164234|lt=Kawasaki, Kanagawa|1=Kawasaki, Kanagawa}} and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a {{ill|Egret|en|Egret|WD=Q3060012|lt=white heron|1=Egret}} flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.
Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).
== Current Status ==
Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}}, cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.

{{coord|35|32|03.8|N|139|39|42.7|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| label =Kabutozuka Kofun| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location Map}}}}

'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} located in {{ill|Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|ja|鶴見区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi-ku, Yokohama|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, though its mound has been significantly damaged and flattened. It is believed to have been part of the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. Although it was constructed in the {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, there is a legend associated with the {{ill|15th century|en|15th century|WD=Q7018|lt=15th-century|1=15th century}} military commander {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan|1=Ōta Dōkan}}.

== Location and Overview ==

In the northwestern part of Tsurumi Ward, on the northern edge of the {{ill|Shimosueyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosueyoshi Plateau|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360955}} (40–50 meters above sea level) facing the south bank of the {{ill|Tsurumi River|en|Tsurumi River|WD=Q7850292|lt=Tsurumi River|1=Tsurumi River}}, multiple {{ill|kofun groups|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} and {{ill|yokoana tomb groups|ja|横穴墓群|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabos|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana tomb groups|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} were once distributed.

The cluster of kofun concentrated in {{ill|Komaoka, Yokohama|ja|駒岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka, Yokohama|1=Komaoka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183049}} is known as the '''Komaoka Kofun Group''', which includes the {{ill|Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|ja|駒岡瓢箪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|1=Komaoka Hyōtan-yama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119217988}}, {{ill|Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|ja|駒岡堂の前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komaoka Dō-no-mae Kofun|1=Q119218105|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218105}}, and {{ill|Iwaseyama Yokoanabo Group|ja|岩瀬山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaseyama Yokoanabo Group|1=Q119218019|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119218019}}. 

Kabutozuka Kofun is located on a ridge to the east of the hills where the Komaoka Kofun Group is situated, at the northern end of the ridge. It is said that there were other kofun besides Kabutozuka in the area, collectively referred to as the '''Kajiyama Kofun Group'''. 

== Legend of Ōta Dōkan ==

In the {{ill|15th century|en|15th century|WD=Q7018|lt=15th century|1=15th century}}, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|WD=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan|1=Ōta Dōkan}} was searching for a suitable hill to build a satellite castle for {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|WD=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle|1=Edo Castle}}. He found the {{ill|Kase Plateau|ja|加瀬台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kase Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kaseyama) in present-day {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|en|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|WD=Q164234|lt=Kawasaki, Kanagawa|1=Kawasaki, Kanagawa}} and spent the night there. During his sleep, he dreamt of a {{ill|Egret|en|Egret|WD=Q3060012|lt=white heron|1=Egret}} flying away with his helmet (kabuto) and dropping it after a while. Upon waking, Dōkan took this as an ill omen and abandoned his plans to build the castle on Kase Plateau. Since then, the plateau has been known as "Yumemigasaki" (Dream-Viewing Hill), and the spot where his helmet fell came to be called "Kabutozuka" (Helmet Mound), which corresponds to the current site of Kabutozuka Kofun.

Although unrelated to this legend, the Kase Plateau in Kawasaki, where Dōkan abandoned his construction plans, contains the {{ill|Kasedai Kofun Group|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65249392}} (also known as the Yumemigasaki Kofun Group).

== Current Status ==

Kabutozuka has long been known as a site associated with the Dōkan legend. Excavations in 1931 and 1938 uncovered artifacts such as {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}}, cut glass beads, and {{ill|earrings|ja|耳環|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/earrings|1=mimikazari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647642}}, confirming that it is a 6th-century {{ill|circular tumulus (empun)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular tumulus (empun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 30.2 meters. Although most of the mound has been destroyed, a monument now stands in front of the remaining portion.
